pages.text: One sunny afternoon, <name> was playing under the big mango tree in her garden when her puppy, Moti, started digging near the flower bed. “What did you find, Moti?” she asked. Out popped an old, crinkly paper!
<name> opened it carefully. “It’s a treasure map!” she gasped. The map had funny drawings — a mango tree, a blue kite, and a temple bell. “Let’s follow the clues!” she said excitedly.
First, they ran to the mango tree. A parrot sitting on a branch squawked, “Squawk! Next clue under the red pot!” <name> and Moti looked — and found a shiny marble!
Then they followed a trail of yellow flowers to the old temple. Ding-dong! went the bell as <name> whispered, “The treasure must be close!”

Finally, behind a row of tulsi plants, they saw a tiny wooden box half-buried in the soil. <name> opened it slowly — creeeak! Inside were colorful shells, marbles, and a note that said: “The real treasure is sharing smiles!”
<name> giggled. “Let’s share them with my friends!” She and Moti ran back home, their pockets jingling with marbles and shells.
That evening, the whole lane sparkled with laughter as the children played together under the glowing mango tree. The treasure had been found — and shared with everyone.
